title: bernardina orientalis
intro: Bernardina orientalis, also known as the Strychnine Tree or Nux Vomica, is a deciduous tree native to South Asia and Southeast Asia. The seeds of this tree contain a highly toxic alkaloid called strychnine, which has been used in medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including analgesic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory effects. However, due to its high toxicity, the seeds are usually prepared in extremely small doses and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional to reap its health benefits, such as relief from muscle and joint pain. The seeds can be prepared into herbal preparations like tinctures, capsules, or extracts, but caution and proper handling are crucial due to the potential for toxicity.
